name: Automated News Newsletter powered by Bright Data

parameters.content:
## Automated News Newsletter powered by Bright Data

### How it works

This workflow runs on a schedule to build an automated news newsletter. It uses Bright Data to scrape Google News, filters the results to the top five stories, then loops through each story to scrape and clean the full article content. After aggregating the cleaned articles, it asks Claude to write a newsletter and sends the final result via Gmail.

### Setup steps

- Configure the Schedule Trigger with the desired newsletter frequency.
- Add Bright Data credentials and configure the Google News scrape target or query in the Bright Data nodes.
- Add Anthropic credentials for the Claude newsletter-writing node and verify the prompt matches the desired newsletter style.
- Connect Gmail credentials and configure the sender, recipient list, subject line, and email body mapping.

### Customization

Adjust the Google News query, the number of stories selected in the filter code, the article-cleaning logic, Claude's writing prompt, and the Gmail recipients or subject line to match the target audience.

## Schedule and select stories

Starts the workflow on a schedule, scrapes Google News with Bright Data, filters the result down to the top five stories, and initializes batch processing for each story.

## Scrape article content

For each selected story, fetches the full article through Bright Data and normalizes the returned content into a clean format before sending control back to the story loop.

## Generate newsletter draft

Collects all cleaned article content after the loop completes and sends the aggregated material to Claude to write the newsletter.

## Send final newsletter

Emails the generated newsletter through Gmail to the configured recipients.

options.system:
=You are a newsletter editor. You write concise, engaging newsletters from industry news articles. For each article you receive, write: - A bold headline (use the article title) - Two or three sentences summarizing the key points IN YOUR OWN WORDS, based on the article content provided - A "Read more" link using the article's link Format the whole thing as clean HTML ready to drop into an email:

for the newsletter title at the top,

for each headline,

for summaries, for the links. Start with a short one-sentence intro before the first story. Output ONLY the HTML. No markdown code fences, no preamble, no explanation.

values.content:
=Here are today's top 5 industry news articles. Write the newsletter.

{{ JSON.stringify($json.data.map(a => ({ title: a.title, link: a.link, source: a.source, content: a.content }))) }}
